reason: Avoid pilot/counterfactual leakage into baseline statistics.
Benchmark rows have much lower success and different provenance.

decision: Use OpenCode/Hermes as crossed interaction cells
reason: Claude Code and Codex are anchor cells, not fully crossed across both models.

decision: Use M1-M4 instead of success-only scoring
reason: Success cannot identify prompt/tool/model interaction causes.

decision: Keep HCI human study out of XAI deck
reason: Phase 4 metrics are evidence materials, not human response data.

decision: Use the 27-slide content draft as the only canonical deck basis
reason: The old 24-slide HTML deck is deprecated and should not drive PPT production.
Its release timing and harness maturity would add unstable variables to this baseline.
Official route behavior is part of each agent product; translation layers would add format confounds.

decision: Use Aider/Exercism Python benchmarks instead of SWE-bench Verified
reason: They are provenance-backed, runnable on the aarch64 host, and better aligned with tool-path divergence analysis.
